title: Slovakian Corn Empanadas
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring cornstarch and encrouted in a flaky pastry, these empanadas are a delicious and energizing snack. They are low in glycemic index and can be enjoyed by people with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cornstarch, flour, and sugar.
Add the cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender to work it into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt.
Add the egg mixture to the bowl with the dry ingredients and stir until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead it a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of corn kernels and a sprinkle of cheese, if using, in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half circle and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with a little bit of egg wash.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
